Case title: Dynamic Surveys with AI-generated follow-ups and cluster reports
Short description: Use dynamic surveys to test a middle ground between structured surveys and interviews, with AI-generated follow-ups and emergent cluster reports.
Empirical unit of analysis: Open-ended survey responses, generated follow-up questions, cluster ratings, stakeholder interviews
Institutional setting: Survey platform / classroom or community feedback setting
Candidate roles tested: Situated interlocutor; context witness; protocol architect
Key typology question: Where does human interaction matter when AI generates tailored follow-up questions but the setting is still a survey?
Observable artifacts / traces: Follow-up prompts, cluster logs, stakeholder interviews, ranking data
Key outcomes observed: Depth of follow-up responses, cluster accuracy, stakeholder utility, respondent experience
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Dynamic surveys produce equivalent depth and usefulness without any human interpretive scaffolding
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Educational settings may not generalize to high-stakes public-policy or vulnerable populations
Why this case has high leverage: Excellent hybrid case because it pressures the line between interviewing and structured surveying.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
